---
title: 'Cofidis'
description: 'Accept payments with Cofidis (4xCard and Cofidis Pay) in Spain through the Paylands API'
---

# Cofidis

With this payment method, the user will be redirected to the Cofidis payment page, where they must provide the required information. In the case of 4xcard, the identity document and the cardholder's card are required, while in the case of the Cofidis Pay product, the identity document and a bank account ownership certificate will be required.

The integration process is [**similar to the redirect payment page**](/guides/accept-payments/web-integration).

```
/payment/process/{token}
```

Cofidis requires an email to be sent in the extra\_data field when creating the order:

```json
"extra_data": {
    "profile": {
      "first_name": "",
      "last_name": "",
      "email": "johndoe@paynopain.com"
    }
}
```

## Integration

To make a payment with Cofidis, follow these steps:

### 1. Generate the payment order on your server

Create the order the same way you would for a [**simple integration**](/guides/accept-payments/web-integration).

The order will be configured with `secure=false`.

As the service identifier, provide the **service\_uuid** of the COFIDIS service configured for one of the two Cofidis products you want, which you can find at **Paylands Backend → Manage → Payment services → [List](https://backend.paylands.com/payment-services/list)**.

If you don't have the Cofidis service activated yet, you should request its activation at **[soporte@paylands.com](mailto:soporte@paylands.com)**.

Once here, we generate the order with the following request:

```
curl --request POST 'https://api.paylands.com/v1/sandbox/payment' \--header 'Authorization: Bearer [API_KEY]' \--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \--data-raw '{  "signature": "[SIGNATURE]",  "amount": 10000,  "operative": "AUTHORIZATION",  "secure": false,  "customer_ext_id": "test",  "service": "[SERVICE_UUID]",  "description": "Payment #1",  "additional": null,  "url_post": "https://my.website.com/process",  "url_ok": "https://my.website.com/ok",  "url_ko": "https://my.website.com/ko",  "source_uuid": null}'
```

Obtaining the following response:

```
{  "message": "OK",  "code": 200,  "current_time": "2022-07-19T13:36:32+0200",  "order": {    "uuid": "997BFC59-2407-4CFD-A518-0FCE478AB7D5",    "created": "2022-07-19T13:36:32+0200",    "created_from_client_timezone": "2022-07-19T14:36:32+0300",    "amount": 50000,    "currency": "978",    "paid": false,    "status": "CREATED",    "safe": false,    "refunded": 0,    "additional": "Paylands test",    "service": "COFIDIS",    "service_uuid": "AB567191-DCCC-4143-81DA-5F1ED5F68B47",    "customer": "test",    "cof_txnid": null,    "transactions": [],    "token": "69694f7765bba3d5d7da3f401d304bef151c8eded8ceac43c48e3b852368502d815c86d0a6e901f031ae13fea256f0ac8d99d7cea7c369b4683b8f7c4db52b95",    "ip": null,    "reference": null,    "dynamic_descriptor": null,    "threeds_data": null  },  "client": {    "uuid": "42B8CF56-A7D7-4D4A-8349-4E27263CB2D5"}}
```

### 2. Redirect the customer to the payment form

We build the redirect URL to the Cofidis gateway using the **token** from the order returned in the previous call.

```
https://api.paylands.com/v1/sandbox/payment/process/{token}
```

We can present the user with a Cofidis payment button like the following:

```
<a href="https://api.paylands.com/v1/sandbox/payment/process/{token}">  <img src="cofidis-logo.png" alt="Cofidis Logo" style="width:200px; height:auto;"/></a>
```

![Cofidis button](data:image/png;base64,...)

When the button is clicked, the user will be redirected to the Cofidis gateway.

![Cofidis Form](/images/docs/apm/assets/images/cofidis-fourxcard-2e2430871a11525867e8777c63674e90.png)

### 3. Enter data and validation code

Once the user has been redirected to the Cofidis page, they will need to enter their personal information and, in the case of Cofidis Pay, select the desired number of installments.

Once the form is completed, Cofidis will determine whether to approve or reject the financing, and the user will be redirected to the OK/KO URL, depending on the result.

### 4. Server notification

Once the payment process is completed, the POST notification will be received, which will be similar to a [**webservice payment**](/reference/#operation/webservice-payment), except for the following details:

* The `source` key that contains the details of the card used, which appears under `order.transactions.[n].source`, will contain the value `null`.

* COFIDIS payments only support `AUTHORIZATION` authorizations as the operative.

## Testing

To run tests in the sandbox environment and force the operation to be OK/KO, you need to consider which COFIDIS product you are using.

For the Cofidis Pay product, you will be asked to complete a form with the information of the user requesting the financing. To successfully complete tests in the sandbox environment, it is recommended to use specific amounts, such as 50000 (500.00€), and to enter "IP+LastName" (IPExample) in the first last name field, in order to avoid the request for real documentation in this test environment.

For 4xcard, you will need to provide a Cofidis user's information and a card. You can use the following cards:

```
OK MC: 5132830960047108 03/24 462
OK VISA: 4977830259527191 03/24 153
KO: 5132830960047199 03/24 813
```

The sandbox URL for Cofidis payments is the following:

```
https://api.paylands.com/v1/sandbox/payment/process/{token}
```
